expressions.expression: I'm afraid I won't be able to...
expressions.meaning: 유감스럽게도 ~를 할 수 없을 것 같습니다
expressions.nuance: 직접적인 'No'보다 훨씬 부드럽고 정중한 거절 표현. 'I'm afraid'가 감정적 완충재 역할을 해 상대방이 덜 상처를 받는다.

dialogue.A: Can you take on this extra project?
dialogue.B: I'm afraid I won't be able to — my plate is full through next month.

expressions.example: I'm afraid I won't be able to make it to the Friday meeting.
expressions.practice: 추가 업무나 요청을 정중하게 거절할 때

expressions.expression: That's not going to work for me.
expressions.nuance: 비교적 직접적이지만 이유를 설명하지 않아도 되는 자신감 있는 거절. 'I can't'보다 주도적이고 명확하다.

dialogue.A: We need this by 8 AM tomorrow.
dialogue.B: That's not going to work for me — can we discuss a more realistic deadline?

expressions.example: A 10% discount is the best I can do — anything lower is not going to work for me.
expressions.practice: 협상에서 조건이 맞지 않을 때 명확히 선을 그을 때

expressions.expression: Let me get back to you on that.
expressions.nuance: 즉각적인 답을 피하고 시간을 버는 표현. 충분히 생각하거나 일정을 확인한 후에 답하고 싶을 때 쓴다. 무조건 OK하는 것보다 훨씬 전문적이다.

dialogue.A: Can you present at our conference next month?
dialogue.B: Let me get back to you on that — I need to check my schedule first.

expressions.example: That's an interesting proposal. Let me get back to you on that by end of week.
expressions.practice: 즉답을 피하고 숙고 시간을 확보할 때

expressions.expression: I have to pass on this one.
expressions.meaning: 이번 건은 패스해야 할 것 같습니다
expressions.nuance: 기회나 제안을 거절할 때 자연스럽고 캐주얼하게 쓰는 표현. 부정적이지 않으며 '이번만'이라는 뉘앙스가 있어 관계를 유지하면서 거절할 수 있다.

dialogue.A: We're having a team dinner tonight. Want to join?
dialogue.B: I'd love to, but I have to pass on this one — I have a prior commitment.

expressions.example: Thanks for thinking of me, but I have to pass on this one.

expressions.nuance: 감사함을 먼저 표현한 후 거절하는 구조. 상대방의 호의를 인정하면서 거절하기 때문에 관계가 손상되지 않는다. 뒤에 이유나 대안을 붙여 쓴다.

dialogue.A: We'd love to have you as a co-founder.
dialogue.B: I appreciate the offer, but I need to stay focused on my current role right now.

expressions.example: I appreciate the offer, but the timing doesn't work for me at the moment.
expressions.practice: 중요한 제안을 정중하게 거절하며 관계를 유지할 때

master_paragraph.text: I appreciate the offer, but I have to pass on this one. I'm afraid I won't be able to take on more projects right now — that's not going to work for me given my current workload. Let me get back to you on any future opportunities, though.
master_paragraph.translation: 제안해주셔서 감사하지만, 이번 건은 패스해야 할 것 같습니다. 유감스럽게도 지금은 추가 프로젝트를 맡을 수 없습니다 — 현재 업무량을 감안하면 맞지 않습니다. 향후 기회가 생기면 다시 연락드리겠습니다.
highlights: I have to pass on this one
I'm afraid I won't be able to
That's not going to work for me
Let me get back to you on that

english_expressions.meaning: 어쩔 수 없이 감수하다, 이를 악물고 해내다
english_expressions.example: It's going to be painful, but we need to bite the bullet and cut the budget.

dialogue.A: Nobody wants to have this difficult conversation.
dialogue.B: We have to bite the bullet — avoiding it is only making things worse.

english_expressions.meaning: 모두가 알지만 아무도 언급하지 않는 문제
english_expressions.example: Let's address the elephant in the room — we're behind schedule and need a plan.

dialogue.A: I think we're all avoiding the elephant in the room.
dialogue.B: You mean the fact that the project is two months behind?

english_expressions.example: We can't afford to cut corners on the safety testing.

dialogue.A: Can we speed up the process somehow?
dialogue.B: We can streamline, but we shouldn't cut corners on quality.

english_expressions.meaning: 같은 생각을 하고 있다, 잘 통하다
english_expressions.example: We're on the same wavelength — let's move forward.

dialogue.A: I think we should prioritize user experience over speed to market.
dialogue.B: We're on the same wavelength — that's exactly my thinking too.

english_expressions.expression: At the end of the day
english_expressions.example: At the end of the day, what matters most is whether the customer is happy.

dialogue.A: We've been debating this for an hour.
dialogue.B: At the end of the day, we need to make a decision and move on.

english_expressions.meaning: 점들을 연결하다, 큰 그림을 보다 / 관련성을 파악하다
english_expressions.example: The data is there — we just need to connect the dots to see the pattern.

dialogue.A: I'm not seeing the connection between these trends.
dialogue.B: Let me connect the dots — these all point to the same root cause.

english_expressions.meaning: 준비 중인, 진행 중인, 곧 나올
english_expressions.example: We have three new features in the pipeline for next quarter.

dialogue.A: What's coming up after this launch?
dialogue.B: We have two major updates in the pipeline — expect announcements in Q3.

english_expressions.example: Nobody wanted to rock the boat, so the problem went unaddressed.

dialogue.A: Should I raise this issue in the all-hands?
dialogue.B: It might rock the boat, but someone needs to address it.

english_expressions.meaning: 생각할 필요도 없는 선택, 너무 명확한 결정
english_expressions.example: Given the cost savings, switching vendors is a no-brainer.

dialogue.A: Should we upgrade to the premium plan?
dialogue.B: It's a no-brainer — the ROI pays for itself within a month.

english_expressions.expression: Put it on the back burner
english_expressions.example: Let's put that feature on the back burner and focus on the core product.

dialogue.A: What about the redesign proposal?
dialogue.B: We'll put it on the back burner for now — it's not urgent.

english_expressions.meaning: 결정을 못 내리고 있다, 중립적인 입장이다
english_expressions.example: I'm still on the fence about the new hire — I need one more interview.

dialogue.A: Have you decided on the vendor?
dialogue.B: I'm still on the fence — both options have strong trade-offs.

english_expressions.example: We jumped the gun announcing the product before it was ready.

dialogue.A: Let's send the proposal today.
dialogue.B: Let's not jump the gun — we should finalize the numbers first.

english_expressions.example: Criticizing him publicly only added fuel to the fire.

dialogue.A: The client is already frustrated. Should we push back on the deadline?
dialogue.B: That would just add fuel to the fire — let's focus on solutions.

english_expressions.example: Don't throw your team under the bus in front of the client.

dialogue.A: The client asked who made the mistake.
dialogue.B: Don't throw anyone under the bus — we own this as a team.

english_expressions.example: We've hit a wall with the current approach — time to try something new.

dialogue.A: How's the research going?
dialogue.B: We've hit a wall. Nothing is working — we need a fresh perspective.

english_expressions.example: The prototype failed testing — it's back to the drawing board.

dialogue.A: The client rejected the entire concept.
dialogue.B: Back to the drawing board then. Let's regroup Monday morning.

english_expressions.example: Let's run the idea up the flagpole before committing to it.

dialogue.A: I have a bold idea for restructuring the team.
dialogue.B: Run it up the flagpole with a few stakeholders first and see the reaction.

english_expressions.expression: Square peg in a round hole
english_expressions.meaning: 어울리지 않는 사람 (또는 것), 맞지 않는 상황
english_expressions.example: He's a creative type in a highly structured role — a square peg in a round hole.

dialogue.A: Why do you think he keeps struggling?
dialogue.B: Honestly? He's a square peg in a round hole — this role isn't the right fit.

english_expressions.example: They've been growing under the radar — now they're a serious competitor.

dialogue.A: Have you heard of that startup?
dialogue.B: They've been operating under the radar for two years — but they're raising a Series B.

english_expressions.example: New regulations aim to create a level playing field for smaller companies.

dialogue.A: How do smaller startups compete against tech giants?
dialogue.B: The cloud has leveled the playing field — anyone can access the same infrastructure.

chinese_expressions.meaning: 말이 도착하자마자 성공한다 → 즉각적이고 완전한 성공을 기원하는 말

dialogue.A: 明天就要开始新项目了，有点紧张。(내일부터 새 프로젝트인데, 좀 긴장돼.)
dialogue.B: 放松，马到成功！(긴장 풀어, 반드시 잘 될 거야!)

chinese_expressions.meaning: 한 걸음마다 발자국을 남긴다 → 착실하고 꾸준히 나아가다

example.zh: 做事要一步一个脚印，不要好高骛远。
example.ko: 일을 할 때는 착실하게 한 걸음씩 나아가야 해, 허황된 목표만 쫓으면 안 돼.

dialogue.A: 我想快点升职。(빨리 승진하고 싶어.)
dialogue.B: 一步一个脚印，先把眼前的工作做好。(한 걸음씩 착실하게, 먼저 지금 하는 일을 잘 해.)

chinese_expressions.pinyin: chī yī qiàn cháng yī zhì
chinese_expressions.meaning: 한 번 함정에 빠지면 한 가지 지혜가 생긴다 → 실패에서 배운다

example.zh: 这次失败了没关系，吃一堑长一智。
example.ko: 이번엔 실패했지만 괜찮아, 실패에서 배우는 거야.

dialogue.A: 上次提案没通过，真失落。(지난번 제안이 통과되지 않아서 정말 실망이야.)
dialogue.B: 吃一堑长一智，下次会更好的。(실패에서 배우는 거야, 다음엔 더 잘할 거야.)

chinese_expressions.meaning: 장점을 취하고 단점을 보완하다 → 상호 보완하다

example.zh: 团队合作就是取长补短，发挥各自的优势。
example.ko: 팀 협업이란 서로의 장점을 살리고 단점을 보완해 각자의 강점을 발휘하는 거야.

dialogue.A: 我们两个擅长的方向不同怎么办？(우리 둘이 잘하는 방향이 달라서 어떻게 해?)
dialogue.B: 正好，取长补短，一起合作效果会更好。(딱 좋아, 서로 보완하면 같이 하면 더 잘 될 거야.)

example.zh: 好的产品经理能博采众长，整合各方意见。
example.ko: 좋은 PM은 여러 사람의 장점을 두루 취해 다양한 의견을 통합할 수 있어.

dialogue.A: 怎样才能做出好的决策？(어떻게 하면 좋은 의사결정을 할 수 있어?)
dialogue.B: 博采众长，多听不同的声音，再综合判断。(여러 사람의 장점을 널리 취하고, 다양한 의견을 많이 듣고 나서 종합적으로 판단해.)

chinese_expressions.pinyin: yī fēn gēngyún yī fēn shōuhuò
chinese_expressions.meaning: 노력한 만큼 거둔다, 심은 대로 거둔다

example.zh: 没有捷径，一分耕耘一分收获。
example.ko: 지름길은 없어, 노력한 만큼 얻는 거야.

dialogue.A: 为什么他比我升职快？(왜 그는 나보다 승진이 빠르지?)
dialogue.B: 你看他平时下的功夫，一分耕耘一分收获。(그가 평소에 얼마나 노력하는지 봐, 노력한 만큼 얻는 거야.)

chinese_expressions.meaning: 현인을 보면 나도 그렇게 되기를 생각하다 → 롤모델을 본받다

example.zh: 见贤思齐，多向优秀的人学习。
example.ko: 뛰어난 사람을 보면 본받으려 하고, 훌륭한 사람에게 많이 배워.

dialogue.A: 你怎么进步这么快？(어떻게 이렇게 빨리 성장했어?)
dialogue.B: 见贤思齐，我一直在观察和学习优秀的前辈。(뛰어난 사람을 본받으려고 항상 훌륭한 선배를 관찰하고 배웠어.)

chinese_expressions.meaning: 아직 일어나지 않은 재앙을 미리 막다 → 사전 예방이 최선

example.zh: 做好风险管理，防患于未然。
example.ko: 리스크 관리를 잘 해서 미리 문제를 예방해야 해.

dialogue.A: 要等出了问题再处理吗？(문제가 터지면 그때 처리하면 되지 않아?)
dialogue.B: 不行，要防患于未然，提前做好预案。(안 돼, 미리 예방해야 해, 사전에 대비책을 준비해야 해.)

chinese_expressions.meaning: 차라리 부족할지언정 함부로 채우지 않는다 → 기준을 낮추느니 없는 게 낫다

example.zh: 招聘要宁缺毋滥，不能为了凑人数降低标准。
example.ko: 채용은 기준을 낮추느니 없는 게 낫다, 인원을 채우기 위해 기준을 낮추면 안 돼.

dialogue.A: 没合适的人，要不要先将就一下？(적합한 사람이 없는데, 일단 타협할까?)
dialogue.B: 宁缺毋滥，等到合适的人再说。(기준을 낮추느니 없는 게 낫다, 적합한 사람이 올 때까지 기다려.)

chinese_expressions.meaning: 만족할 줄 아는 사람이 항상 즐겁다 → 지족(知足)이 행복의 근원

example.zh: 知足者常乐，不要总和别人比较。
example.ko: 만족할 줄 아는 사람이 항상 즐겁다, 항상 남과 비교하지 마.

dialogue.A: 我总觉得自己不够好。(난 항상 내가 충분하지 않다는 느낌이야.)
dialogue.B: 知足者常乐，你已经做得很好了。(만족할 줄 알아야 행복해, 너는 이미 충분히 잘 하고 있어.)
